# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a multi-job GitHub Actions workflow where one job builds artifacts and subsequent jobs download and deploy them. The deployment job should download artifacts from the build job and execute deployment scripts. This is for our multi-stage deployment pipeline.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Multi-job GitHub Actions Workflow with Artifact Downloading & Deployment Job 
on: [push] # or manually trigger the workflow on a schedule, e.g., every night at midnight (0 0 * *)  
jobs:
  build_artifacts:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest    
    steps:      
      - name: Checkout code        
        uses: actions/checkout@v2 # or use the new version of 'actions' which includes a more robust `github.token` authentication method, so it can be used to authenticate your GitHub Actions workflows and run jobs on repositories using SSH keys (if you have any)        
      - name: Build artifacts      
        # Add steps here for building the project or generating build assets         
    outputs: 
      job_build_artifacts.token1234567890 ${{steps.job_run_on_ubuntu-latest.outputs['job_build_artifacts.return']}}   # Use this output in the next step to run on a specific OS version (Ubuntu, etc.)
  download:   
     needs: build_artifacts     
     runs-on: ${{needs.build_artifacts.outputs['job_run_ubuntu-latest']['token1234567890']}}   # Use the output from previous job to determine which OS version (Ubuntu, etc.)   
     steps:      
      - name: Download artifacts       
         uses: actions/download-artifact@v2  # This action downloads a single or multiple files based on its id. In this case we're downloading 'job_build_artifacts.*'.  
          with:          
            path: ./path    # The directory where the downloaded artifact will be placed, e.g., './downloaded-files/.'  (optional)    
      - name: Deploy to target environment       
         run: |      
             echo "Deployment script here"   # Add your deployment scripts and commands in this area
